quality.rationale: 涉及四维速度分量求导与电磁场张量洛伦兹变换，需跨惯性系应用功率公式解释表观能量变化

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s4_4.13_c.md]4.13 (c) Problem 4.13. A particle of charge $q$ , mass $m$ , moves in a circular orbit of radius $R$ in a uniform B field $\mathbf{B}_{\underline{\mathbf{e}}_z}$ . (c) Calculate $\mathrm{du}^{0^{'}} / \mathrm{dr}$ and thus $\mathrm{dP}^{0^{'}} / \mathrm{d}\tau$ . Explain how the energy of the particle can change since the B field does no work on it.
origin.answer: $\frac {\mathrm {d} u^{0^{\prime}}} {\mathrm {d} r} = - \frac {\beta \omega \gamma u^{y}} {m \sqrt {1 - \omega^{2} R^{2}}}$, $\frac {\mathrm {d} P^{0^{\prime}}} {\mathrm {d} \tau} = - \frac {m \omega \gamma u^{y}} {\sqrt {1 - \omega^{2} R^{2}}}$；在运动参考系中观测到感应电场 $\mathbf{E}^{y'} = -\gamma \beta \mathbf{B}$，该电场对粒子施加洛伦兹力做功，从而改变其能量。
origin.solution: (c) Since $\mathfrak{u}^{0'}$ is not constant, energy is seen to increase:$$\frac {\mathrm {d} \mathrm {p}^{0^{\prime}}} {\mathrm {d} r} = \mathrm {m} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathrm {u}^{0^{\prime}}} {\mathrm {d} r} = - \frac {\mathrm {m} \beta \omega \gamma \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {y}}} {\left( 1 - \omega^{2} \mathrm {R}^{2}\right)^{\frac {1} {2}}}$$In the moving observer's frame he observes an $\underline{\mathbf{E}}$ field $\mathbf{E}^{\mathbf{y}'} =-\gamma \beta \mathbf{B}$ . (This is easily computed by Lorentz transforming $\mathbf{F}^{\mu \nu}$ .) The observer should then expect to see work being done on the particle at a rate$$\frac {\mathrm {d} \mathrm {p}^{0^{\prime}}} {\mathrm {d} r} = \mathrm {qE}^{\mathrm {y}^{\prime}} \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {y}^{\prime}} = - \frac {\mathrm {m} \omega \gamma \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {y}^{\prime}}} {\left( 1 - \omega^{2} \mathrm {R}^{2}\right)^{\frac {1} {2}}} = - \frac {\mathrm {m} \omega \gamma \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {y}}} {\left( 1 - \omega^{2} \mathrm {R}^{2}\right)^{\frac {1} {2}}}$$This resolves the "paradox."
